name: Tex-Mex Chili Meatballs with Zesty Tomato Sal
recipeIngredient: 1 can (4 oz) Mild green chilies, drained and chopped
1 ¾ cups Fresh bread crumbs (about 4 slices of bread)
recipeInstructions:
Zesty Tomato Salsa:
1. In a skillet over medium heat, heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil.
2. Add onion and chili powder; cook about 10 minutes, stirring frequently until the onion is tender and coated with chili powder. Remove the onion to a bowl.
3. To the bowl with onion, add beef, egg, chilies, bread crumbs, ½ tablespoon each of Monterey Jack and Cheddar cheeses, and salt. Mix well.
4. Shape mixture into 1¼-inch balls.
5. In the same skillet over medium heat, cook the meat mixture for 15 minutes, turning frequently until well browned on all sides and cooked through.
Main Instructions:
1. Preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
2. Heat remaining 2 tablespoons vegetable oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
3. Add meatballs; cook for 15 minutes, turning frequently until well browned on all sides and cooked through.
4. Meanwhile, place tortilla chips in a single layer on a jelly-roll pan; bake for 10 minutes until crisp and golden.
5. To serve: Place meatballs in the center of a large serving platter. Sprinkle with remaining Monterey Jack and Cheddar cheese. Arrange tomato wedges and lettuce around meatballs if desired.
6. Serve with tortilla chips and salsa. Makes 4 servings.
Zesty Tomato Salsa:
1. In a saucepan over medium heat, heat 1 tablespoon vegetable oil.
2. Add red and green bell peppers (diced), onion (diced), and garlic; cook about 10 minutes, stirring frequently until tender.
3. Stir in fresh ripe tomatoes (diced) and hot red pepper sauce for 1 minute longer until heated through. Makes about 1½ cups.
